user_reviews_extended.Name: Monique
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Came here with a friend on Saturday and the food DID NOT DISAPPOINT. Everything we tried was really good. I got the Godzilla Roll and Curry Fried Rice. The rice had a nice kick to it but it wasn’t too spicy and the Godzilla Roll wasn’t spicy at all.

My friend got the General Tso’s Chicken which was really good and crunchy. She also got the crab cakes which were nice and filling with no filler! Highly recommend coming here. Best restaurant in Laurel. I’ll be back. My waitress Cindy was really nice. The ambience is really nice and the restaurant is beautiful.

They also have these in house barbecue chips which were good as well.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Sher Nell
user_reviews_extended.Description:
So, I was introduced to Blue Sunday Bar & Grill one Saturday evening. Upon entry, I quickly took the opportunity to adore the aesthetic of this place. To me, it was giving a chic, vibrant, yet modern aesthetic.

When we arrived, it was a fairly busy/packed evening, however, we were seated within 15 or so minutes. Within a couple of minutes of being seated, our waiter introduced himself and gave us some honest insight on some of the dishes we had interest in, which was greatly appreciated.

For our appetizers, we first had the fried catfish, which was fried/breaded perfectly. However, the catfish was lacking the oomph I needed via a little more seasoning. We also had the Louisiana Gumbo, which was flavored very well. For this dish, I really did appreciate the waiter’s disclaimer that the gumbo is really good, however, it’s not exactly like an actual gumbo made in Louisiana.

On to the entrees. Unfortunately, the entrees took a very long time to come out, which was definitely a test of maintaining my composure under hunger.  So, by the time our food came out, there was added build up to try/immediately devour our dishes. For my main dish, I ordered the ribeye steak (which was cooked at a medium) with some asparagus. Similar to the fried catfish, I felt that the ribeye also needed more seasoning, however, the sauce that came with this dish added a unique flavor that made the ribeye enjoyable. The asparagus on the other hand were made to my liking.  My friend had the General Tso chicken with fried rice which was simply okay.

Overall, I had an average experience here.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Nekisha Hill-usry
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Stopped in during a visit to town for business and had an absolutely fantastic happy hour experience. The crab bites were amazing—fresh, flavorful, and perfectly prepared. The drinks were well-crafted with beautiful presentation, making happy hour feel upscale and intentional.
The lamb chops were nothing short of magnificent—tender, seasoned to perfection, and cooked just right. And the dessert? Dashing, indulgent, and the perfect ending to an already stellar evening.
Excellent atmosphere, great attention to detail, and a must-visit spot when you’re in town. I’ll definitely be back!

user_reviews_extended.Name: Nas B.
user_reviews_extended.Description:
We recently visited Blue Sunday and had a pleasant experience, primarily due to the good service. The staff was incredibly friendly, attentive, and made us feel welcome throughout the meal—definitely the highlight of the evening.

However, the food was just okay. I ordered the Singapore Noodles, which were okay because, and the Salmon Rolls, which were also fine. I also tasted the Asparagus, which was underwhelming. My friend had the Lamb Chops and commented that they were okay, but not standout.

Blue Sunday is a great spot if you value a pleasant atmosphere and high-quality service, but the menu execution needs to be sharpened to match the prices and ambiance.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Jen Powell
user_reviews_extended.Description:
I was super excited to check out this restaurant. I’ve visited their other locations and always sat in the bar/lounge area — it’s comfortable, relaxed, and a great spot for mingling or catching a game. You can also dine in the main dining area if you prefer a more traditional setting.

The bartenders were very attentive and made sure our glasses stayed full, which I really appreciated instead of having to constantly ask.

They have a nice happy hour menu — and it’s offered on the weekends too!

The food is very flavorful, with a fun American/Asian-inspired twist.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Carrie
user_reviews_extended.Description: This location is larger than the Bowie location! Great food & great service. However they have to do something about the doors. While the inner doors are cute, they were often left open and the cold draft had many guests freezing cold. Our table asked that at least 1 of the 3 people standing at the reception area ensure the door remained closed. Below are the beef short ribs and a birthday brownie.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Teresa Stewart-Buck
user_reviews_extended.Description: My first and last visit.  The service was not worth it, food was basic, my bar drink was horrible and $110 gone.  I met up with three girlfriend's and we all had the same thoughts on the food, drinks and service. The atmosphere was rushed and not customer friendly.   The restaurant felt like a fancy looking hospital cafeteria.  We were told twice of a limited dining time (by host staff & server).  I observed that other tables who were seated and eating upon our arrival sat past the limited times.  I recommend for the purpose of counsumer feedback that management meet with guests and identify the needed feedback.

Hi Teresa,
 
Thank you for taking the time to share this, and I’m genuinely sorry your first visit felt rushed and unwelcoming. Being told about a limited dining time (and seeing inconsistency in how that was applied) is frustrating, and that’s not how we want a night out with friends to feel.
 
If you’re open to it, please email the Laurel store directly with the date/time of your visit and any details you’re comfortable sharing so our management team can review and follow up.
 
— Blue Sunday Team

user_reviews_extended.Name: Larry “Big Schlim” Mcleod
user_reviews_extended.Description:
There is a popular restaurant in Bowie I’ve been to a couple of times, you probably have too!! Well, they opened another spot in Laurel (Maryland), and I pulled up!! I heard the decor was everything!! Let’s GO!!
.
Where I am: @bluesundaylaurel
.
Location Reviewed: 3443 Ft.Mede Rd, Laurel M.D.
.
Items Reviewed:
LIKE THAT!! Their Complimentary BBQ Chips. Tasty, crunchy wavy chips!

LIKE THAT! Their Wings ($16 eight fries wings with their Mumbo sauce). Nicely crispy, fried hard wings with a spicy Mumbo sauce!!

STAMP!  Their Cheesesteak Egg Rolls ($16 with steak, cheese, & onions). A crisp wonton shell, cheesy, & meaty!!

STAMP!! Spicy Curry Fried Rice ($19 with chicken, shrimp, veggies, & rice). A good balance between mildly spicy, & flavorful, with plenty of protein!!

LIKE THAT! Their General Tso’s Chicken ($21 with broccoli, & fried rice). Spicy, well fried, & saucy chicken, with fresh broccoli!

STAMP!! Their Lobster Scallop Medley ($36 with grilled lobster, & pan seared scallops with a creamy cajun sauce, & asparagus). Well seasoned buttery lobster, the asparagus needed a lil bit more flavor, & the scallops were a tad fishy…DELICIOUS.

STAMP!! Their Tiramisu ($16 layered espresso, & mascarpone cake). Sweet, velvety, & tasty!!
.
Customer Service: On Point.
.
Parking: Decent.
.
Recommendations:
.
Accessibility: It’s is wheelchair friendly, & it’s differently abled persons friendly

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️➕=LIKE THAT
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️=DELICIOUS
⭐️⭐️⭐️=DECENT
⭐️⭐️=PASS

user_reviews_extended.Name: God's Girl
user_reviews_extended.Description: This place is top tier. The ambiance and decor are beyond fabulous. The place simply looks like money. The walls are textured different dining layouts amazing architecture. Lots of gold and brass the lighting is  crazy gorgeous.The bar is huge and actually has 2 islands behind the bar from Asian and American entrees to delectable appetizers. The stuffed shrimp cheese steak Eggrolls crab dip Philadelphia roll  and chowder did not miss. Our server Brittany was excellent. She was extremenly attentive. The place is beautiful. Cute birthday performance Drinks are excellent. I had the Blue Sunday signature drink and my friend had the Coconut Mojito, excellent. Gorgeous queen working the bar with a big afro, gave it that added touch. I also had the salted caramel vanilla cake it was wonderful. Definitely sufficient staff all in sync with headsets. This place has a private dining area and stage. The design of every section is amazing. This is s very classy place. Great for wonderful date night, out of town guest, entertaining colleagues, I will be back and they have a rewards program. Only draw back is the floor is slippery I was dressed so I was wearing heels, I had to skate along lol other than that I recommend to those 5 ***** top tier crowd looking for elegance good food and ambiance.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Xavier S.
user_reviews_extended.Description: I attended the soft opening and the atmosphere is top notch. Plenty of space in the main dining area and the bar area is amazing! The amount of seats in the bar area is a plus. Food was good. I tried the happy hour menu Rock Shrimp, Old Bay Wings and Fries. Only downside, unable to try their alcoholic beverages due to them just now getting their liquor license, but I was advised by my server that they should be doing alcohol in about two weeks. Overall this is definitely impressive and has the potential to be here for a very long time. I will definitely come back!

user_reviews_extended.Name: Raven Ciera
user_reviews_extended.Description:
I went to the soft opening for an appetizer. I ordered the shrimp trio. It was delicious. Cheat code: Ask for the sweet chili dipping sauce instead of the sauce it comes with.  My sister ordered the chicken tacos and said they were very good.

The servers are still learning so be sure to check the accuracy of your bill before you pay.

The decor is beautiful. The private room in the back is super cute. The bar isn't open yet, but I can see this place being a new "hot spot" in Laurel once it's fully open later this month.

She did run into some people who were rude but still kept a respectful manner while fulfilling our needs. She did great, and made sure we were great. Shout out to her and Blue Sunday for making sure my uncle had a great b day brunch!

user_reviews_extended.Name: Tyrone S.
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Review for Blue Sunday (Laurel, MD)

I was excited to try Blue Sunday since it just opened in Laurel, and my experience was a mix of highs and lows.

One thing that stood out was the staggered delivery of food. Plates came out at different times, so some of us were eating while others were still waiting. It made the meal feel a bit disjointed, and I hope they work on timing so groups can enjoy their food together.

My garden salad was a letdown—the greens were too soft, almost like eating cooked spinach but cold. On the other hand, the salmon salad was excellent. The lettuce was crisp and fresh, and the salmon was cooked perfectly.

I also tried the lemon pepper chicken wings, which took a while to come out but were worth the wait. They weren’t overly salty or greasy, the grease tasted fresh, and they arrived hot, crispy, and flavorful—just right.

A couple of surprises to note: soft drinks don’t come with complementary refills, and there’s an automatic 18% gratuity added to the bill. Neither is a dealbreaker, but it’s something to be mindful of.

The aesthetics of the restaurant are beautiful, and the staff was friendly. Even with a few bumps in execution, I think Blue Sunday has real potential. With better food timing and more consistency across dishes, it could become a great spot in Laurel.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Ciera P
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Exceptional service from start to finish!

Navi and Vee made our experience at BlueSunday absolutely outstanding. From the moment we sat down, they were warm, attentive, and genuinely invested in making sure we had a great time. Their knowledge of the menu was impressive, their recommendations were spot-on, and the service felt effortless without ever being intrusive.

They anticipated our needs, kept the energy fun and welcoming, and handled every detail with professionalism and care. It’s rare to find service that feels both polished and personal, but Navi and Vee delivered exactly that.

Because of them, BlueSunday isn’t just a restaurant we enjoyed—it’s a place we’ll intentionally return to. Huge thank you to Navi and Vee for setting the standard for what exceptional hospitality looks like.

I only am rating 3 stars based on this restaurant is already highly reviewed vs my personal experience.
I want to mention that the ambiance and atmosphere were wonderful and everyone looked like they were having a fabulous time and enjoying their food.

Although that being said, the night we dined, our food wasn't fantastic. In my opinion, the crab dip was 80% dip and 20% crab, and there was sooo much dip- to the point where I felt like they could have priced it a few dollars less since we finished the bread and hadn't made much of a dent in the dip.

We ordered buffalo and old-bay wings, and I could understand if the old-bay wings were slightly salty, but both orders were very heavy on salt. I was still hungry, but didn't feel right finishing it.

Drinks were amazing, our server was so sweet. I really want this place to thrive so just wanted management to see this and can make sure the food stays consistent.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Madeleine
user_reviews_extended.Description:
I tried the shrimp tacos and the crab cake bites, and both were really good—well seasoned and enjoyable. However, the guacamole was a letdown. It didn’t taste like fresh avocado and felt off compared to the rest of the food.

As for the drinks, I wasn’t a fan. Every cocktail I ordered had a strong artificial, syrup-based taste. I understand that cocktail mixes are commonly used, but the flavor wasn’t pleasant and took away from the overall experience.

The atmosphere, on the other hand, was very nice and welcoming. The space felt upscale, but more like an expensive buffet than a restaurant where you’re looking for a curated dining or cocktail experience.

Overall, the food had some highlights, but the drinks and overall dining experience didn’t fully meet expectations.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Ariel Fowlin
user_reviews_extended.Description: Decided to visit the restaurant because I saw good reviews on TikTok. We decided to go yesterday (President's Day) for Happy Hour, it was very busy in the bar area and there were only two bar tenders working. Our bartender was very friendly but you could tell he was definitely overwhelmed. We observed several groups of people get up a leave because they were never greeted at their tables. It took a while for us to receive our drinks, a couple that came in well after us was served their drinks before us. The dark skinned guy that they had running food from the kitchen to the bar area was extremely rude, slamming plates down and giving major attitude as if he did not want to be there. He even got into a back and forth with my husband. The food was decent. I may give this restaurant a second try but it will definitely be during the middle of the week before Happy Hour begins. Do not visit this restaurant when it is crowded! Oh, and the restaurant was very cold inside like there was a draft and we were sitting nowhere near the door.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Narcisse Aspilaire
user_reviews_extended.Description: I had the worst service experience at any restaurant I’ve ever been to. I was seated at a table for almost 30 minutes, and I watched servers pass by multiple times without even offering me water or utensils. When the hostess finally came over, I informed her that I had been waiting for almost 30 minutes and hadn’t been served or addressed. I then observed that people who had come in and been seated in less than five minutes were being served immediately. Finally, a server with dreads came to my table and asked if I was ready. I told him that I had been waiting for over 30 minutes and had never been served. He responded rudely, saying, “You ready now,” with an attitude. I had no choice but to take my things and leave the restaurant. This was my first time there, and it will definitely be my last.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Kitty Wu
user_reviews_extended.Description: My first time here came on a Saturday around dinner time and it was packed, definitely make reservations ahead if you’re dining here on a weekend- table wait times were an hour long for two, we got lucky and noticed a couple leaving the packed bar and snagged their seats. One of the bartenders (no idea who he was) had a bit of an attitude and seemed annoyed when I asked to put in our food order, also didn’t explain what the specialty cocktails were at all when I asked what was in them (suggest listing it out on the HH menu). Ambience is great, interior looks classy and modern, and the front desk hostesses are always friendly and polite. HH deals are great and love that they have all day Sunday HH, and HH on Saturday as well. Food and drinks are good. Tried the Godzilla roll, flatbread, shrimp risotto. Now it’s my go to local bar- Q is my usual bartender, he’s great and makes a mean henny sidecar! Most of the staff offer great service. Great place to watch games too since they have a big bar and 4 TVs spread out.

A huge shoutout to Lilly, Vee, and Brayden… they truly made the night.
Lilly was warm, attentive, and made sure everything at the table was perfect without ever feeling rushed.
Brayden brought such good energy, had great drink recommendations, and checked in just the right amount.
Vee behind the bar was amazing.  Fast, friendly, and every drink came out perfectly made.

The whole team worked together effortlessly, and it honestly made the night feel special. Great food, great atmosphere, and even better people. If you’re coming to Blue Sunday, definitely hope you get one of them you’ll be in good hands! Especially them drinks! I recommend the henny peach for
Sure!!

Will absolutely be back. 😌🍽️🍸
user_reviews_extended.reply_text_original:
Hi Trib,

Thank you so much for the amazing review! We're thrilled you had such a great experience, and we’ll be sure to pass your shoutout along to Lilly, Vee, and Brayden — they’ll love hearing this. We're glad the food, drinks, and atmosphere all hit the mark. Can’t wait to welcome you back for another great night (and another Henny Peach)!

user_reviews_extended.Name: Tish Thompson
user_reviews_extended.Description: Decor inside is beautiful lighting is perfect tables offer plenty of room. Food however needs some work. My 1st time I ordered Singapore Noodles. The noodles were not Rice noodles and were more like Angie hair pasta. I also asked if it could be made spicy and our waiter said yes. However when it was served and not spicy at all. The waiter offered to bring out some hot sauce which is not the same.  2nd visit we ordered Lemon pepper wings. The wings had no lemon or pepper flavor but they were fried well and very crispy just forgot to season them.  My entree this time was General Taos Chicken again asked to be spicy and again no spice. It was tasty with the fried rice but definitely could have been better.  I will try Blue Sunday 1 more time. Hoping for the best

user_reviews_extended.Name: m payne
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Beautiful place, seems to be off to a great start with customers. Food: the table BBQ chips cheapen the experience for me given the ambiance and the prices of the food; my order came out wrong,  the lettuce of the salad tasted old. The Louisiana gumbo was surprisingly tasty with turkey sausage, I do wonder if the casing was pork or beef though. The heat was appropriate but could have had more seasoning. Service: The servers were all very mannered and very kind. My bill had a different price than what was quoted and I did not eat most of my meal due to the questionable freshness. After inquiring about the full bill given to us and discussing our experience with the server,  i received a 10% discount ($1.60). When i asked for the manager, we  waited for over 25min so we finally left disappointed.
Atmosphere: music was very loud, they could have kept it moderate before 9p or 10p, in my opinion, then turned it up for the all adult crowd later. There were several families with kids that could have possibly appreciated that and a kids menu.
I wanted to love this place but apparently I was not there on the best day... yet still paid the 18% gratuity which was included in the bill. Go figure..

6:30 Saturday night in January
not taking reservations

Bar area open seating- booths and cocktail tables included.

Ended with a martini and playoff football.
18% gratuity included on check.
Will be back!

I had a great experience at Blue Sunday! Braden was absolutely amazing — professional, friendly, and very attentive from start to finish. He made sure everything was perfect and went above and beyond to provide outstanding service. It’s rare to come across someone who genuinely cares about the customer experience, and Braden definitely does. I will definitely be back!

user_reviews_extended.Description: Unfortunately, my experience at Blue Sunday Bar and Grill was disappointing. The customer service was not good at all. user_reviews_extended.Name: marie franklin
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Don go when it’s busy. The waiters seem as if they never had a waiter job and only worked at a fast food. I asked three ppl one of three that bought what I asked for. Chopsticks and soy sauce. Our waiter never came back to check if our food was good was everything correct or even did I get what I had asked the previous person. Our waiter also took 30 min to bring my friend drink. Also after the initial  meet but aid she would come back to take our orders that was 15-20 min later. I’m not in a hurry to go back
To the Laurel location.

user_reviews_extended.Description: Blue Sunday Bar & Grill in Laurel, MD is a must-try! Conveniently located on Fort Meade Road, this beautifully decorated spot features stunning light fixtures, comfortable seating, and an inviting atmosphere. The staff is exceptionally friendly, and the food is not only well-seasoned but also presented with care. A visit here is absolutely worth it.

user_reviews_extended.Name: D. Turner
user_reviews_extended.Description:
We went around 5:30 on a Saturday. It wasn't busy; lots of open seating. We loved the artwork painted on the walls and the music. Once we were seated, no one came to take our drink orders for about 10 mins, until one of the servers stopped by and asked if we'd been helped. He was very kind and swift with our orders, even allowing my spouse to practice ordering in Spanish. We got birria tacos and the salsa verde burrito (we think; it was a limited-time item). You get unlimited chips and salsa, too, and whew that salsa was yummy.

Both were absolutely delicious! The food came out super quick, too. For dessert, we got the fried ice cream and blueberry cobbler cheesecake. Both a hit!

Although I got a cocktail, I won't comment on it because I usually don't drink alcohol because I don't care for it, so my opinion on whether it is good or not is skewed.

Would definitely go back if in the area!

user_reviews_extended.Name: Rich Man
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Pleasant experience, the atmosphere is very welcoming. Staff is polite, very knowledgeable about the menu. My server, Darian helped me pick. He was paitent,listened to my request and recommended El Mata Hambre
because I told him I liked steak. The Host dress very professional and nice. Very polite. My wife loved her birria tacos! A lot of space, so its good for dates, birthday's etc. Bathroom sink has a cool mixed paint type of feel too. A lot of thought was put into this restaurant. Finally, the salsa that comes with the chips are phenomanal...like seriously! "Well recommended" is an understatement for this beautiful hidden gem. Definately coming back for my wife's graduation. We love this place😇

user_reviews_extended.Name: monear qoqandi
user_reviews_extended.Description:
It was a pleasant experience, the atmosphere was nice and welcoming.

The host and waiters was super friendly, super nice. Tgey helped with choosing from the menu made our experience enjoyable.

The restaurant was tidy and clean so I'm giving them 10/10 on that.

Talking about the food: I ordered,
● Chiken Wings: was below average, very sad.
● Nachos Brava: was dilecious.
● Birria Tacos: was good not the best. Needs more seasoning.
● Cajun Jambalaya Pasta: sweeter than expected, and I feel the sauce is a bit broken.
● Chef Go Burger: the seasoning was on point but unfortunately the patty was over cooked.
● Surf N Turf Burger: not good at all and to much souce! Why?
● Pulpo Asado: too expensive for a bit over overcooked octopus that was served cold on a hot platter. At least the charred parts were good! It comes with beans and rice, the rice was mushy. It was not worth the price.
● The fries was really really really bad.

Food quality was ok and unfortunately survise was really slow, food came cold and we were only checked on after the appetizer and then nothing.

Anyways the waiter was nice got too busy, that I couldn't wait for the bill anymore and went to pay at the intrance.

Would I come back? NO, I would NOT.

1/10 do not recommend.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Claudia Reyes
user_reviews_extended.Description: Honestly, I'm never going back to this place. It's a complete and utter mess, disgusting. Starting with the owners and managers, it's total chaos. The waiters and food vendors just walk by and drench you in sauces and drinks. It's a total disarray. They should be more organized. I went and spent 400 with my friends, and it turns out the managers don't even know how to ask permission to walk through the crowd. What a disgusting place, and what a mess! And on top of that, they charge an entrance fee! It's a restaurant, not a nightclub. They should have a limit on the number of people allowed inside and at least be aware of social distancing. It's such a disorganized disaster. I don't recommend it at all. I'll never spend money in this pigpen again, because that's what it looks like. If only they were more organized and professional. So, if you're looking for a good time, I don't recommend it. You'll just waste your valuable time and money.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Shaar
user_reviews_extended.Description: This was our first time to this restaurant and we went for Mother’s Day dinner. Our servers were polite and friendly. We tried 3 different entrees and 2 different desserts. We enjoyed all the food. The only thing I will say is that both the carne asada and steak tin tan were too chewy but had great flavor. If they fix that they will be 5/5 stars. The creme brûlée was good but the blueberry cheesecake was the star between the two. I would also suggest adding more lighting outside for the patio seating so it’s more lit and inviting. Overall a great experience and atmosphere, will definitely be coming back.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Pete Neforos
user_reviews_extended.Description:
This is based on a few previous experiences I had at the establishment; before last night, I enjoyed going in there and having dinner, even though it’s like 8 bucks for a beer and it gets very packed, my gf and I had a nice dinner and enjoyed the entertainment as well!

Last night, I went there to meet my gf, got there before her and they wanted a 15.00 cover to have dinner and drinks.  The dinner and drinks are already uncomfortably overpriced and I was puzzled as to why I was asked for 15 at the door.  Though I enjoyed the atmosphere in prior visits, I’m not going anywhere I have to pay 15 bucks just to sit down and eat.  This place got greedy and I’m not even convinced EVERYONE was charged the cover.  NO THANKS.  Gf and I went elsewhere.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Natalia Ferus
user_reviews_extended.Description:
We went for the soft opening to #SalsaBrava today which is in the same space as Chibal (miss you!) used to be on route 1 across from IHOP. We enjoyed our time there. The sangria was good but a little light on the booze for my taste. The coconut mojito was good but a little too sweet for me. My son got a carne asada to see is it’s anything as good as #MangosGrill, and it was very good. The cook was medium just like requested and it was flavorful. Different flavor from Mangos but good. Came with the salad that I enjoyed. I got a fajita - only type they have- with bacon wrapped shrimp, steak and chicken. The steak was good, chicken was ok but I never like chicken in restaurants. The shrimp were a little bland for me and needed some flavor as they were wrapped in bacon with Oaxaca cheese which is very mild itself and bell peppers. In my opinion the shrimp has to be seasoned better. But the cook on the shrimp and steak were good. The beans were OK and the rice was ok for me as well. We also got a aode of garlic fries and really wmjpyed them, they had actual garlic pieces on them which we like. For dessert we tried beignets and they were good though half of them was hotter than the other half which makes me think they were microwaved. Anyways, this place is definitely better than La Fogata that was in that place for a minute, but not as good as Chibal at its prime. We definitely will try them again.
The service today was good, everyone was training which is understandable, but everyone was friendly and polite. I’m sure it will take a few weeks for them to get into the swing of things, but definitely give them a try.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Delia Frazer
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Today, Friday, September 26, 2025, my friends and I decided to check out Salsa Brava after work for Happy Hour. It was my second visit—my first had been during a quiet weekday, so I hadn’t seen what it's like during busier times. Everything started smoothly until more Latino and Hispanic customers arrived. Since the restaurant is primarily Spanish and Latino cuisine, it wasn’t surprising to see a lively crowd. But what disappointed me was how suddenly we felt invisible. No one came to take our order or asked if we needed anything. The bartender who initially served us was moved to the floor to be a waiter, however the Bartender at the bar, refused to serve us claiming we needed to speak to the original bartender, who was now busy waiting tables but we were sitting at the bar.

When we finally caught his attention, he asked another male bartender to make my drink—unfortunately, it was the wrong order. I was willing to make it better and still drink the drink if someone gave me pineapple juice, so I asked two bartenders and a waiter for some, but they all ignored us.

It was very evident once the restaurant’s Latino/Hispanic customers arrived in larger numbers, our presence was disregarded.

Poor service, dismissiveness, and what felt like racial bias made it clear we were no longer welcome. I want to support businesses that value all their patrons and offer genuine hospitality.

To my Black and Brown friends, let's choose to uplift places that serve us with respect and quality service every time and not when it's convenient for them.

It was my second visit—my first had been during a quiet weekday, so I hadn’t seen what it's like during busier times. Everything started smoothly until more Latino and Hispanic customers arrived. Since the restaurant is primarily Spanish and Latino cuisine, it wasn’t surprising to see a lively crowd. But what disappointed me was how suddenly we felt invisible. No one came to take our order or asked if we needed anything. The bartender who initially served us was moved to the floor to be a waiter, however the Bartender at the bar, refused to serve us claiming we needed to speak to the original bartender, who was now busy waiting tables but we were sitting at the bar.

When we finally caught his attention, he asked another male bartender to make my drink—unfortunately, it was the wrong order. I was willing to make it better and still drink the drink if someone gave me pineapple juice, so I asked two bartenders and a waiter for some, but they all ignored us.

It was very evident once the restaurant’s Latino/Hispanic customers arrived in larger numbers, our presence was disregarded.

Poor service, dismissiveness, and what felt like racial bias made it clear we were no longer welcome. I want to support businesses that value all their patrons and offer genuine hospitality.

To my Black and Brown friends, let's choose to uplift places that serve us with respect and quality service every time and not when it's convenient for them.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Edwin M
user_reviews_extended.Description: I’ve been here a few times, This place is amazing! The food, the drinks, and the atmosphere were all great, and I had probably the best waitress I’ve ever had. The vibes were incredible. Unfortunately, my fourth visit to this establishment was extremely disappointing. After waiting approximately 30 minutes for my table—which I did not mind—I approached the two women at the front to confirm that my table was ready. They began whispering to each other, and I believe one of them was the manager. The manager then informed me that there was a $15 entrance fee.This was surprising, as I have visited this place three times before on the busiest nights and have never been asked to pay an entrance fee. Despite this inconsistency, I paid the fee for myself and for the four guests who accompanied me.What was most upsetting, however, was that the group in front of us was not charged any fee. I also waited to see if the party behind us would be charged, and they were not. I asked several other people waiting in line whether they had been charged an entrance fee, and all of them said no.This inconsistency and lack of transparency completely ruined my experience. I am extremely disappointed that my party was the only one required to pay, without any explanation. For this reason, I am giving this establishment a rating of 2, and I will NOT be returning.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Nik C
user_reviews_extended.Description:
We were seated at the same time as another set of black women and I internally hoped they weren’t just putting us in the corner to be forgotten. Here is our experience.

Waited 15 mins for a server, 25 for water, 35 for 1 appetizer, didn’t get utensils or napkins until we asked. The women next to us received their food with no utensils or napkins and had to wait 10 mins to get the server back. We were only checked on twice, never had an opportunity to order more drinks, get more water or make changes to our food. (This is true of both tables).

Food: Not good. I had the fried snapper and my partner had the fajita. Rice was bland, beans were bland, fish was overcooked and all of her proteins were flavorless. The women next to us asked for salt and pepper which tells me they felt the same way.

The DJ was rocking. I’m a New Yorker so I LOVE Latin music and he gave me home vibes. The atmosphere was lively but everything else was subpar. I wanted to love it but the food and service were enough to prevent me from returning.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Taty Esther
user_reviews_extended.Description:
I’m giving 1 star solely based on the predatory service of this place.
The food was pretty good.

My issue is that we came at night, ordered water and were given Fiji water -clearly just to be charged. Why in the world would a restaurant serve you Fiji water if you didn’t ask for water in a bottle —let alone Fiji?

Second, we ordered two appetizers. They were out of one. Instead of just taking it off the bill and letting us know they were out, the server replaced it with a seperate appetizer and kept the charge —again, who does that?

It was late at night on a Saturday so it’s clear they see people drinking and think they ate stupid, but I really despise predatory service like this. I prefer open and honest service.

If you refuse to serve us glasses of water (why in the world would you do that?) —then communicate it that you only have Fiji water.

If you run out of an appetizer, communicate and let the paying customer decide if they want to swap it or just remove it.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Franklin Villanueva
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Update: went recently with a group of friends on a Thursday evening and it was great. Sat at the bar with Jenny as my bartender and her service was AMAZING 10/10. She was friendly, attentive and gave great customer service. Based on this experience, I will be back. Food was great this time too, I had the wings and it they were saucy and tasty!

Visited recently to check out the scene and see if the rebrand from La Fogata was worth it. Keeping it simple: it wasn’t.

I did arrive about 30 minutes before closing, but that’s no excuse for what happened. At exactly 2 AM, a staff member (security) came to our table and, without saying much, began pulling the chairs out from under us while we were still seated (yes, this actually occurred!), literally lifting them to get us to stand up. They then repeatedly told us to “get up and get out” because they were closed. They tried to play it off as friendly, but what’s friendly about sitting at a dinner table, and having your chair pulled and lifted to get you to leave?

Never in my life have I experienced something so blatantly rude and disrespectful at a restaurant. If this is how they treat guests at closing time, the rebrand missed the mark entirely. Safe to say that we will not be returning. We didn’t even get to try any food or drinks. Very dissatisfying experience.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Dawn Sall
user_reviews_extended.Description:
⭐ 1 Star – Unfortunately Our First Visit Will Be Our Last

This was our first time trying this restaurant. Upon entering, we immediately noticed how clean everything looked and smelled — which always makes us happy and hopeful. Our server was friendly, and the atmosphere felt welcoming.

We ordered alcoholic beverages and the Bravo Platter for two, which gave us a sampling of all the meats. The food arrived hot and tasted good.

So why just one star?
Unfortunately, soon after eating, both of us began experiencing severe stomach pain and diarrhea — for hours. The only thing we shared that day was the food from here, so whatever was borderline bad… was bad enough to ruin our night and our health. We were practically fighting over the bathroom.

It’s disappointing because the service and cleanliness were promising, but we won’t be trying this place again.

I visited Salsa Brava last Wednesday, and it was a great experience! The place has a cozy, friendly vibe with upbeat Latin music playing in the background. The staff were welcoming and made sure we were taken care of the whole time.

We ordered the Mar y Tierra plate, Parrillada, Carne azada and everything was absolutely delicious – well-seasoned and cooked perfectly.
Prices were reasonable for the quality and portion sizes. Everything felt like a good deal.

Overall, I’d definitely come back. Great food, great service, and a warm atmosphere. Highly recommended!

title: BJ's Restaurant & Brewhouse
description: Family-friendly chain outpost pairs familiar pub fare with an extensive list of house brews.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Dcraider 00
user_reviews_extended.Description: Dropped in on a Wednesday evening bc I was hungry and we needed to avoid the rush hour traffic. Glad we decided on this location. Everything here felt upscale except the bill at the end, so I was quite pleased with the overall experience. Popped in during happy hour and left even happier. I grabbed 5 peppered bbq wings for just 5 bucks and the taste was amazing. I washed them down with their oasis amber brew which was quite refreshing. My wife ordered the beef dip sandwich that came with fries and slaw. Also very well prepared and seasoned. I ordered the pizookie meal deal, which consisted of a personal pepperoni pizza and Caesar salad that ended being the largest Ive ever been served. Great food, Great value, Great service! We will definitely make a return trip in the very near future.

user_reviews_extended.Name: S. L.
user_reviews_extended.Description: Food was very affordable and pretty good. You get a lot for what you pay for but there is nothing special about it. Dessert, on the other hand, was amazing! Overall, not a bad place to grab a bit to eat. Service was only OK. The young waiters and waitresses were very new at serving and need a lot of training. Didn't take dirty empty plates walking by, on their phones a lot, holding things in an unsanitary way. For example, holding a cup from the top (where the mouth goes) and handing me a lid with a greasy thumb print on the inside. Nothing that a manager can't fix if they know it is happening. But I didn't see a manager on the floor so things couldn't be corrected.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Phace Artistry
user_reviews_extended.Description:
I ordered the deep dish pizza from BJ’s in Laurel and it was very hearty and filling. The crust was thick and buttery with a nice golden bake, holding up well under all the toppings. The pizza was loaded—pepperoni, sausage, and ham were generous, so you definitely get your money’s worth if you like a meat-heavy slice.

Flavor-wise, it was savory and rich, but on the greasier side, which is pretty expected with deep dish. The cheese was melty and abundant, though I would’ve loved a bit more balance with the sauce—it could’ve used a touch more seasoning or tang to cut through the richness.

Overall, it’s a solid deep dish option when you’re craving something heavy and indulgent. Not an everyday pizza, but perfect for sharing or when you want comfort food that really sticks. ***Mondays Large Pizza is $20

user_reviews_extended.Description: Hands down, great food and great service! The food was hot and fresh, you could tell it hadn’t been sitting under a heat lamp. I’d come back just for that alone! Overall, 10/10 for both service and food. We will definitely be back!

My son complained of his burger he only ordered the patty, bun, cheese and bacon,  the order came wrong, it went back but it came back the same they only added the bacon, we are not stupid!  it had to go back. Overall the food was not that great, we will be visiting other restaurants.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Norshina Oneal
user_reviews_extended.Description: My family and I decided to celebrate my mom’s birthday here. We had a reservation so there wasn’t much of a wait time. Because I arrived later than my party they were already seated with drinks and have ordered food which was of course no problem. When I arrived food was being delivered to some of the people at the table again not an issue. The issue is when the waiter took over 10 minutes to acknowledge the 3 extra people that were at the table. As my sister received her plate she noticed the broccoli was not cooked. It wasn’t just cold it was literally not cooked. As she flagged the waiter down to give him back the plate he didn’t apologize he didn’t try to make it right nothing just took the plate and walked off. After my kids and I ordered and received our food the table began to talk and of course eat. The waiter never returned asking if we needed refills if the food was ok. It was almost like he avoided the table all together. By the time we saw the waiter again he was bringing to go boxes to the table which is a little weird and rushed considering he didn’t ask if we wanted desert nor bother coming back to the table at all. My brother ordered desert in which the ice cream of it was supposed to be orange the waiter never came to the table stating the restaurant did not have all the ingredients just bought the desert and put it on the table and said we don’t have any more orange ice cream. My sister went to find the manager and speak to her as she wasn’t present on the floor during our visit. As a food service worker myself I understand the behind the scenes chaos that most guest do not however,  what I do not understand is poor guest service. It does not matter what is happening in the background it is your job to provide a service and this restaurant did not do that. The price point of the food is too high for such poor service and under cooked food. If you are reading this review do yourself a favor and skip this establishment

user_reviews_extended.Name: A. McCullough
user_reviews_extended.Description: I went during happy hour today, and there weren't many people there.  I also was not greeted upon entry, nor after I sat at a table in the bar area.  So, I went to the bar, where I had to move three times before I could find a clean spot. Eventually, the bartender asked me if I wanted a drink.  While fixing my drink, he dropped an orange on the counter and then still put it in my drink!  Luckily, I saw him and reminded him that one of the pieces of fruit had fallen on the counter. After giving me a cold stare, he immediately fixed me another drink that he spilled all the way to the counter and all over the counter. Amazingly, it was still full.  Since it was one server for the entire bar area, he shuffled between the actual bar and the seated patrons. He fixed drinks, rang up orders, counted cash, collected credit cards, emptied boxes, put away glasses, texted on his cell phone, carried out food, chatted with co-workers, and then put fruit in drinks with his bare hands.  One would think that the restaurant would require him to use tongs for the fruit, since he is touching all of these surfaces.  My food was good, but expensive, and I wasn't given any silverware. There oven was also broken, so there were many items on the menu that patrons could not order. In addition, the beer battered pork ribs are no longer on the happy hour menu, and they no longer come with fries.  The bartender told me that fries were $3, but I was charged $4.15 on my bill.  The ribs were nearly $15.   I also learned that I could taste as many of the beers as I like because BJs brews all of their beers.  I recommend Berry Blast which is listed as a cider; it tastes like Moscato wine.  I really enjoyed trying it, and it only has 3% alcohol.  I tried Jeremiah Red, which tasted more like beer, but not as strong.  It was also served in a crack glass. Then, I tried a third, Moon something that tasted like champagne. Though I had to flag the bartender down each time I had a question or needed something, he pleasantly provided it.  When I asked for my check, I wasn't given a pen.  The vibe of BJs has definitely changed since its original opening, and I would not say that it is a good one.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Chaz Sparrow
user_reviews_extended.Description: First time eating at BJ'S restaurant and it was just so so. Soon as we walked in we were greeted. The hostess was nice and polite. She takes us to a table close to the bar and the guy at the bar who I'm guessing is the bartender is rude! He says to the young lady I can't take them I'm too busy. First off there was only 3 tables so I guess he just couldn't handle anymore. Then he says we'll i guess they can sit here but they'll be waiting a while before I serve them. What!? So I say, no thanks cause I wouldn't have tipped you anyway to his face. Food was bland and had no taste at all. My pizza (phots shown) was good. My mother's fettuccine alfredo with chicken had no taste and very little chicken. My wife's pizza didn't even have all the toppings on the pizza. My sister got a burger and she said it was fine. My son had the fish and chips and the fish fillets were really small. I'll give BJ'S another chance just not this location!

user_reviews_extended.Name: Mike Johnson
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Overall: Professional waiter, decent drinks, incredible appetizer and decent main course.

Drink: Arnold Palmer - 7/10. Lemonade was a little bitter, just a little bit more sugar but still good.

Appetizer: Pot stickers -10/10. Amazing crisp and sauce was very complimentary.

Entree: Bistro Burger w/o bacon - 10/10. Burger was made medium perfectly. All flavors really merged well.
Parmesan Chicken - 2/10 chicken was dry, Parmesan was good. Whatever butter sauce was supposed to be there was nonexistent or just got absorbed by the cheese, but didn’t aid in the chickens dryness. A Alfredo or marinara or really any kind of thicker sauce would’ve complimented the chicken a lot better. Only bad part of my visit.
Would come again, but definitely not order the chicken.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Moxie
user_reviews_extended.Description:
We stopped in here for lunch. I ordered the Deep Dish Ziti and it was very greasy. It was so oily that I was able to pour the oil out of the little cast iron skillet that the ziti was served in. If we make a trip back, I plan to stay clear of this dish.

The highlight of the meal was one of the appetizers: the fried couscous balls.  These were reminiscent of a mac & cheese ball and came with a dipping sauce that was a bit difficult to actually dip anything into as it seemed to require a knife to spread it.

Overall, the fried couscous balls were yummy and great to share. If you get the ziti, just make sure your arteries can take the beating.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Colin Shorter
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Great place for a good sit down meal with a plethora of food options ranging from hearty steaks to sodium friendly salads.  Their prime rib sundays are to die for.

Our service was a bit on the slow side but our server and her trainee were beyond polite and accommodating to my dietary restrictions.

Only recommendation to the owner: music was a bit loud and was a bit hard to talk with my company, albeit was good music tho.  Correct that and it will be a shinning 5 star review!

user_reviews_extended.Description:
I came here a few years ago and the deep dish pizza used to be something BJ’s was actually known for. Now? The quality has completely fallen off. The ingredients taste cheap—like something straight out of a Domino’s box, not a real restaurant.

And the worst part? There wasn’t a drop of pizza sauce on it. Who serves a “pizza” with no sauce? It was ridiculous and honestly a waste of money. I’d never order this again.

This place has gone downhill. It was a giant disappointment. The place was pretty much empty, we ordered take out and they told us it would be 20-25 mins.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Janine D
user_reviews_extended.Description:
The service was amazing. CC (Courtney) was very nice and attentive. I was looking forward to the crispy calamari because it was perfect the last time I had it at this restaurant. This time is was not very crispy and the pieces were much thicker than the last time. The Cherry Chipotle Salmon was delicious. The asparagus was great with the white cheddar mashed potatoes, very well seasoned. The Southern Comfort Chicken Sandwich was also pretty good. The fries were amazing.
The monkey bread pizookie was tasty but the presentation  was terrible. There was nothing to pull apart, as small pieces of shriveled up bread were thrown into the container, already separated. Overall, it was an average experience. I've definitely had better food here before and hopefully next time will be better than this time.
The atmosphere was casual. We had a lot of privacy in a booth but there was also a bar for more casual diners. There was a college football game on and everyone was into it. It was a nice vibe.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Shawn Papi
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Bold Flavors, Big Vibes – BJ’s Restaurant & Brewhouse in Laurel, MD

As a frequent guest, BJ’s Restaurant & Brewhouse in Laurel, Maryland never fails to deliver a satisfying, social dining experience. It’s the kind of spot that blends hearty, comfort-forward plates with a lively atmosphere—great for family nights, casual business dinners, or friends catching up over shared plates.

What makes it stand out
Hearty, crave-worthy menu: From signature BJ’s classics to creative pizzas and robust entrees, there’s something for every craving. The bold flavors and generous portions hit the spot every time.
Great beer pairing options: A wide craft beer selection and house brews provide easy, tasty pairings for any dish, plus specialty flights for the beer-curious.
Consistent quality and value: Reliable flavor, well-prepared dishes, and fair pricing make it easy to enjoy a satisfying meal without overspending.
Friendly, efficient service: The staff are easygoing, knowledgeable about the menu, and quick with recommendations or substitutions to fit dietary needs.
Comfortable, family-friendly vibe: The space is lively yet approachable, perfect for a casual night out, a family dinner, or a weekend catch-up with friends.

Signature favorites I love
Bar-style apps and shareables: Wings, loaded nachos, or spinach-artichoke dip for a great starting point.
Crowd-pleasing entrées: Burgers, pastas, and premium pizzas that are reliably flavorful and filling.
Desserts and coffee finishes: A sweet endcap to a satisfying meal.

Ambience and experience
Casual and inviting: The atmosphere blends a sports-bar energy with a family-friendly dining room, making it easy to relax and enjoy good food with good company.
Great for groups: Large tables, quick service, and a menu that scales well for groups of all sizes.

Practical tips
Try the Brew-Your-Own-Pizza option or a chef’s special for something unique to the night.
Check for happy hour or daily specials to maximize value.
Reserve ahead for larger groups to ensure seating and timely service.
Save room for a dessert like their famous pizookie for a甜-tastic finish.

Bottom line
BJ’s Restaurant & Brewhouse in Laurel, MD is a dependable, crowd-pleasing choice for hearty American fare with a craft-beer twist. It’s ideal for casual family dinners, a lively night out with friends, or a relaxed business lunch where good food and a lively atmosphere matter. Highly recommended for anyone who loves bold flavors, generous portions, and a welcoming, bouncy dining experience.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Rose Benitez
user_reviews_extended.Description: I've been here a total of 3 times. The food is mediocre and the staff is slow. The to go team typically looks unkempt and the manager I dealt with today took over 20 minutes to figure out how to refund a 6 pack of root beer. The handcrafted root beer is typically really good, but somehow they were out. I heard a staff member mention no one ordered it. I had to ask the manager for a refund for it because after telling me they were out of root beer he just stood there and stared at me, like I was supposed to have the answer to this issue. During my dine in experience the wait staff was polite but the kitchen took forever. I waited almost an hour for a pizza and then another 20 minutes to order the dessert, which took yet another 30 minutes to get. The prices are not cheap enough to warrant these kind of experiences. For two people you're looking at $50 to $60. This place is basically a crappier version of an Applebee's. No wonder each time I've been here it's been empty.

user_reviews_extended.Description:
Went last night for the first time.  Our waitress Taylor was GREAT!  Seating is Bbw/bhm friendly.  A few ppl were there with kids and some passionate people about the game.

Even though it was busy it did not take long for us to get our meals. The food was great my date and I had the pork chops, spinach, mashed potatoes.  I had the chicken broccoli Alfredo.  We shared the strawberry shortcake and it was 🔥🔥. Drinks of course were good.

Parking depends on time I had to park by the mission across the walk it’s not far. My date was able to get a parking spot in the lot on the side of the restaurant.

We plan to visit again, nice experience🌸

user_reviews_extended.Name: Customer Review
user_reviews_extended.Description: Very disappointed! I have visit the BJ Brewer and BOWIE as well and I am very disappointed with that location as well. My family did a carryout order last night. I will never visit a BJ Brewers again unless I am ordering pizza when a Monday when it is 50% off. That is the only thing they seem to be able to fix right. The service is awful. They never come out to curbside at either location and you have to go in to get your food. Then there is a long wait because the employees are eating the food and joking and playing with each other. The management is poor when it comes to customer service and running the store. I have spent lots of money here, but I think this is it for me. Our order was over 60 bucks last night and it was so not worth it. That food was awful. My family had to eat the wrong food because we live a ways from the Laurel location and we wasn’t going back. I had to eat my cold food because I had to take my medication.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Maureen Pedrick
user_reviews_extended.Description:
We arrived after 8 and there were many tables available. A woman walked up to us older lady she asked how many we said two She yelled at the young man who seemed startled and told him to take us and he refused. He said he already had three or four tables some inside and outside. She looked at us and told us to go in the other direction and said she would find someone who would take care of us.

The waitress we had was very nice no issues with her at all she even laughed at my husband‘s terrible jokes. and she apologized multiple times for the Wait and issues with the food. My hair wasn’t even my dermatologist

The drinks and the dinner took forever to get my husband got his meal 10 minutes before I got mine.  I had a salad with very overcooked steak why would that take longer than a cheeseburger and fries?The dessert took over 20 minutes to get.  The entire time we were there, they were probably only eight tables inside that had customers and two couples at the bar.

I did see more employees outside walking around, Talking to each other and one came from the back and sprawled across a booth seat, put her hat down over her eyes and took what looked like a little map.
user_reviews_extended.text_original:
We arrived after 8 and there were many tables available. A woman walked up to us older lady she asked how many we said two She yelled at the young man who seemed startled and told him to take us and he refused. He said he already had three or four tables some inside and outside. She looked at us and told us to go in the other direction and said she would find someone who would take care of us.

The waitress we had was very nice no issues with her at all she even laughed at my husband‘s terrible jokes. and she apologized multiple times for the Wait and issues with the food. My hair wasn’t even my dermatologist

The drinks and the dinner took forever to get my husband got his meal 10 minutes before I got mine.  I had a salad with very overcooked steak why would that take longer than a cheeseburger and fries?The dessert took over 20 minutes to get.  The entire time we were there, they were probably only eight tables inside that had customers and two couples at the bar.

I did see more employees outside walking around, Talking to each other and one came from the back and sprawled across a booth seat, put her hat down over her eyes and took what looked like a little map.

user_reviews_extended.Name: charlie lady-gee
user_reviews_extended.Description: March 17, 2019, I ordered through Grubhub at 655pm w/ETA 7:32 to 7:45pm Food arrived at 8:07pm.  Im righting this re view at 8:15pm. I first get a call from Grubhub saying my order that I paid for, was not received nor prepared.  I ordered the 5 meat pizza. It has toppings missing from about 1/3 of the pizza. Dominoes pizza is made better and much cheaper. And the pizza was no longer hot at delivery, rather cool to the touch. I also ordered 2 sparkling raseberry lemonades with no ice. Both cups are filled with ice and neither drink is sparkling Its just a large cup mostly with ice, some lemonade and at the bottom of the cup is a light colored rasberry syrup. Again no sparkling added. The spaghetti with meat- balls looks extremely dry. l'll have to prepare and add my own sauce. The spaghetti was supposef to feed 4 people, and had only 8 meatballs and about 2lbs of thin angel hair spaghetti.  At $70+ dollars including a 20 % tip , tax and delivery fee . I expect better food and drinks from BJs.  Honestly, I deserve a full refund.  All the food was wasted bc it was so bad, no one wanted to eat.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Kee
user_reviews_extended.Description: Pur 1st going here, and it wasn't a good experience.  We were greeted at the door ( the hostess was very nice), she sat us at a table. We waited 15 minutes before a waitress came over. She took our drink order and took a while to come back. When she finally came back, we placed an appetizer order.  Ok received that, and she disappeared for another 20 minutes. I had to ask another waiter if he could find her for us.  Our drinks were messed up, and we had to research order those. We ordered a pizza half cheese and half pepperoni. The pizza comes, and we noticed it looked weird.  Once again, our waitress was nowhere to be found. We decided to try a slice and uuck! The pizza was dry, no sauce on the pizza, they crust looked as if it was wheat. We couldn't eat the pizza.  We finished our drinks and had to ask the hostess if she could find our waitress so we could pay our bill. The waitress finally came over and asked how the pizza was? Mind you were took a bite and couldn't eat it. We said it was dry and nasty.  She just smiled and said, "Oh,you ready to check out? I was confused because she didn't offer to get us another pizza or even ask us if we wanted anything else. We paid our bill and left. We won't be returning here again. Definitely a  waste of our money!!

user_reviews_extended.Name: Adam Harper
user_reviews_extended.Description: My family and I went to BJ’s in Laurel for dinner. We have three children from 2 years of age to 11. The way she catered to our every need, including making our kids laugh, bringing refills without having to ask us, and making sure our food was delivered hot and exactly what we ordered! I wish we could have a server like Leila at every restaurant we visited. Kudos to BJ’s for hiring someone like Leila who is organized, efficient, and truly gives quality service. I believe individuals like Leila, value good work ethic and pride themselves in providing an exceptional dining experience.

user_reviews_extended.Name: devyn
user_reviews_extended.Description: It was my very first time coming to this restaurant and I ended up having a really bad time. My group of friends and I came here as a celebration for a dance competition and it’s normally tradition to come here but unfortunately we now have to break this tradition. We placed reservations a month in advance but we ended up having to sit on opposite ends of the table from the rest of our party. We had no server for about 45 minutes and our service was pretty okay with her but the worst part was the food. We ordered our food when it was still light outside but we got our food when it was pitch black outside. I ordered my food first and waited 2 hours just to get it served last. There were missing parts of everyone’s meal like missing sides, chicken, and meatballs gone from pasta. This location had some of the worst service ever. We only got a discount and no apologies were ever given. This restaurant doesn’t deserve the reviews it’s been given.

user_reviews_extended.Description: Was invited out to the bar for the first time and didn't know what to expect I was pleasantly surprised that there was karaoke and it was pretty good! Very enjoyable evening! The place was packed when I got there probably close to 10:30. I lucked out and got a table with several seats in the karaoke area after a gentleman saying and him and his friends all left afterwards. It seemed to be an ebb and flow to the crowd throughout the night so you could get a seat at different times. The other side is a bar and there's also some tables on the side. And also on the karaoke side there are several tables and a pool table in the back and one loan little couch lol. I didn't see the regular menu but they even have a late night menu that's pretty decent. They have specialty drinks but can probably make just about anything.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Michael Mansaray
user_reviews_extended.Description:
This is hands-down my favorite bar in the area! The wings here are absolutely unbeatable—seriously, some of the best I’ve ever had. And if you haven’t tried their fried deviled eggs, you’re missing out. Trust me, they’re amazing.

The bartenders here know how to make a drink right. They don’t skimp, and the drinks are strong, just how I like them. It’s the perfect vibe with a DJ most nights to keep the energy up and the place jumping.

Karaoke nights (Monday, Wednesday, Friday, Saturday, AND Sunday) are always a blast. Whether you’re singing or just watching, it’s non-stop fun. Plus, for football fans, they have Long Island pitchers for just $16 on Sundays. What more could you ask for?

If you’re looking for great food, great drinks, and great vibes, this place is it. Highly recommend!

user_reviews_extended.Name: Torae E.
user_reviews_extended.Description: My mom and I found this place from Google since we were in the area and it did not disappoint. We walked in and there wasn’t a host so you can seat yourself and order at the bar. Jack was a great bartender and was very attentive despite the bar being crowded. The food came out very fast and it was fresh and tasty. We got wings, fries, and steak and cheese eggrolls and the eggrolls were perfect and the chili sauce set it off! The wings were perfectly cooked and seasoned. The drinks were a perfect pour and well balanced. I will definitely be back!

user_reviews_extended.Name: MJ Buns
user_reviews_extended.Description: I visited this place with my husband and it was a wonderful experience. It was refreshing to have a server cater to what you want. It was easy to order. I told her I am a light weight and right away she said she will make my drink with less alcohol and my husband's drink was strong so she added more cranberry juice. We were able to get the wings how we liked it. The ambience was one of easy going and everyone in there was having a good time. The bathrooms were so clean and "homey" as my husband described it. I did not feel uncomfortable using it as it is a public bathroom. In a time where the world is just crazy, it feels good to know there is a place where you can go and get exactly what you want, how you want. It is definitely a "hideout" from reality.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Whay Whay
user_reviews_extended.Description:
Stay away from the hideout if you want a drink and a bite to eat. It is chaotic and significantly understaffed! Ordering a drink requires much effort and involves jockeying for position to get the one bartender's attention, which takes a long time. Good luck. Oh, and the one bartender also serves the food for the entire place, so there is no one at the bar half the time. So bad.

And don’t even make an effort to order food. Ours never arrived after waiting for an hour.  We finally had it and checked out; of course, our food order was on the check and had to be removed because, as I said, we were waiting for an hour and got nothing!

There are much better places that are adequately staffed, where you don’t have to fight to order, and where you actually receive what you ordered. Imagine that.

The food is great! Fried deviled eggs, wings, and quesadillas are my usual go to's. The vibe is soul served on ice with a good splash of hip-hop. My advice? Go. ♡♡♡

Excellent food, nice selection of music and overall good vibes socially.
Live entertainment is also great.
The cook, Rich, really knows how to mix it up in the kitchen. Once you get a taste of his cooking you'll definitely come back for more. Also would like to mention that the bartenders are the best; very cordial, always greet you with a smile on their faces, good at mixing up unwatered down drinks,  and are pleasantly humorous not to mention beautiful inwardly, as well as outwardly. The HideOut is highly recommended.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Ayesha Speaks
user_reviews_extended.Description: Obviously, I haven't been here in months due to COVID. This is my review based on previous experiences. If you are an AA female it is very hard to get a drink, even on a slow night! They will treat you like you don't exist! I've even tipped them before ordering just to get a drink. I wish they would hire some MALE bartenders! Sometimes I will ask a guy to order for me. I'm only giving them 2 stars because of their fried deviled eggs and music; otherwise it would be none! I don't understand how you can be rude to someone you don't even know. Needless to say, this bar is my LAST resort.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Carolynn Steele-Law
user_reviews_extended.Description:
I'll start with service: It was absolutely amazing. There were two servers and both were quick, friendly, and super attentive. They came to check on us multiple times, always made sure our drinks were filled, and asked us how things were after each item. They were great.

The food was pretty average for the most part. Nothing special, but not terrible, and I felt it was reasonably priced. Typical "cheap bar food". We got three apps to try (wings, tater kegs, fried mushrooms). The wings were crispy and the buffalo sauce was mild with a lot of flavor. The tater kegs were pretty good, aside from a few bites of overly chewy bacon, but the mushrooms tasted a little off and the batter was pretty burnt - we ended up only eating a couple and asked them to toss the rest. We also got cheesesteak, a burger, tater tots, and fries. The cheesesteak and burger were both pretty good - juicy, flavorful, and cooked well. The tater tots and fries were similar to standard bagged frozen fries/tots you can get at any grocery store, but didn't taste bad.

Overall, it was alright. Not sure if I'd go back for the food, but the people who worked there seem really great, and I could see why people would be regulars.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Jake Pretot
user_reviews_extended.Description: It's been probably a decade since visiting Oliver's. While the outside of the building has been painted and no longer white, the inside is still extremely dated and in dire need of a facelift. There wasn't any live music when we visited on a Friday night. Food was good. Burger was tasty, but bun was very cold like they just took it out of the refrigerator. The bar staff is extremely professional and provide great service. If you are looking for a bar with Keno, sold food and reasonable drink prices, they have it covered!

user_reviews_extended.Name: Katherine Wilkening
user_reviews_extended.Description: This place. The homemade honey mustard. And THE FAMOUS GREEN BEANS. EPIC. Service was absolutely amazing; we were just staring a diet (keto) and we told the bartender/waitress. She recommended the grilled chicken breast ( definitely recommend regardless of if you are on a diet or not). We got the green beans as a side....OH BOY. They are buttered green beans, seasoned, with bacon and sautéed onions. 😍😍😍. I got a side of the homemade honey mustard for my chicken— I would put that stuff on anything. Not to forget, the bartender served the entire room of 20ish people attentively!! Would come back to Oliver’s anytime. 10/10.

user_reviews_extended.Description: This was my first time at Hot Pot and it definitely won’t be my last! The decoration and atmosphere was nice and fun. The music was really enjoyable and the seating was spacious and clean. The server was nice and walked us through everything and they even have directions on how long to cook things on a small cheat sheet. The food was fresh and delicious. user_reviews_extended.Name: Amal Adnan
user_reviews_extended.Description:
I’ve heard of this spot and I wanted to check it out!

First of all, the color displays are just beautiful walking in!
This restaurant has an open space with a nice bar and tables everywhere that has grills built into them!

The whole idea is to order a variations of meats, vegetables, and seafood, grill them on the table or cook them in hot pots of soups and enjoy!

You’re actually given an iPad to pick items that you want to eat from the menu.
There are different variations of sauces and seasoning to your liking.
Some meats on the menu are seasoned before they come out.
I was impressed with how the meats, seafood and vegetables were fresh!
You also have a salad bar and sauces station.

FYI! You could potentially order a little too much if it’s your first time, so be careful with what you’re ordering because you CANNOT take your leftovers home.
Also, because you are grilling and cooking remember you will smell like food when you come out.😂😂 but this is all part of the fun!😂😂

This is a fun place for the family, dates and even friends gathering!
I would highly recommend!

I wanna give a shout out to our waiter, Derek, who was extremely helpful and smiled all the time!

user_reviews_extended.Name: phillip phelps
user_reviews_extended.Description:
I had to take a moment to leave a review for one of the best servers I’ve had in a long time, DEANDRE F. at K-Pot on Montrose Ave.

Today I came in to enjoy some time with a coworker and his service truly made the experience outstanding. From the moment we sat down, DeAndre delivered five star service that elevated the entire visit. His attention to detail was impressive and his hospitality was genuine and welcoming.

He was very attentive without hovering, kept our table perfectly maintained, and stayed on top of everything with smooth pre bussing. Nothing ever piled up on the table, drinks were refreshed right on time, and everything flowed effortlessly. He even brought over a custom seasoning mix and made sure our grill and table stayed clean so we could relax and enjoy ourselves. That level of care does not go unnoticed.

DeAndre’s attitude was warm, upbeat, and professional. You can tell he takes pride in making sure guests have a great experience. His service made the whole visit more positive and enjoyable for everyone at the table.

K-Pot is already a cool spot, but DeAndre made it even better. If you visit, definitely ask for him. We will absolutely be back.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Michelle
user_reviews_extended.Description:
We had a great time at KPOT! We loved the service, the atmosphere, and especially the spacious booths and table setup. Ordering on the tablet made everything super easy and convenient.

The sauce bar was large with lots of options, which we appreciated, but a few of the sauces (like the peanut sauce and soy sauce) were pretty watery and didn’t have much flavor. Since sauce is such a big part of hot pot, it brought down the experience a bit for us. We were so disappointed in it area.

The KBBQ and all-you-can-eat aspect were really enjoyable though, and overall we were disappointed the sauces didn't hit, as we loved so much of the rest of the experience.

Even if it cost a bit more and you got better sauces that would be more worth it.

Debating if we would come back. It probably wouldn't be our first choice but we did really like it!

user_reviews_extended.Description: 2 hours of unlimited food. The food was good (better be since you're cooking it). user_reviews_extended.Name: Pepc
user_reviews_extended.Description: Kpot Laurel is my second kbbq experience (my first being kpot Rockville). The selections for the ayce were similar to that of Rockville including the buffet selection. They even provided free ice cream sandwiches after our meal. Our server was also very nice. However compared to the Rockville location they used a different ordering software that I found to be more limiting. The main problem being the lack of a server call button. I had to wave both my arms in the air just to call my server over. They also used butter instead of oil for the bbq, which while not bad for taste did make it a little difficult to grease the grill. They also had the grill overhead very close making it a little awkward to use. All in all a perfectly fine establishment for a fun time with friends and affordable location compared to others such jongro in Annapolis.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Justine Sade
user_reviews_extended.Description:
We had an incredible dining experience at tonight while celebrating my Cousins birthday! From the moment we walked in, the atmosphere was vibrant, welcoming, and fun. We came in as a party of 7 and we were seated immediately. The food was absolutely delicious — fresh ingredients, flavorful broths, and an amazing variety of options for both hot pot and Korean BBQ. Everything was well-stocked, clean, and perfectly prepared.
A very special thank you to our server, Andy, who truly made our visit exceptional. Andy was attentive, friendly, knowledgeable, and made sure we had everything we needed throughout our meal. His positive attitude and excellent customer service really stood out and made our experience even more enjoyable.
If you're looking for a place with great food, great vibes, and outstanding service, KPOT is definitely the spot. We can’t wait to come back!

user_reviews_extended.Description:
Had dinner with my family tonight and we had a great time. This was our first time experiencing a Hot Pot restaurant.

Food: Food was plentiful, fresh and arrived quickly. My only suggestion would be to have a system so that plates that contained raw meats can be discarded off the table immediately after use.

Wait staff: Our host and waiter were extremely accommodating and provided clear instructions on the ordering process.

Atmosphere: Tables, utensils and diningware were clean and there was a nice ambiance/vibe.

Overall, our experience was nice, I just wish they would remove the plates quicker.

user_reviews_extended.Name: Man
user_reviews_extended.Description:
I feel disappointed with this location compared to other locations.
The staff members used scissors instead of proper tools to remove the grill, which is unhygienic and unprofessional. The scallops were still frozen when served for grilling, showing poor food handling. Portions were small for a group of four, and did not match expectations.
Service was noticeably less friendly than other locations. The grill was replaced while still wet, unlike other location where it is clean and dry. To make matters worse, the restrooms were dirty, poorly maintained, and had a strong odor.
This location seriously needs to improve hygiene, service standards, and staff training.

user_reviews_extended.Description: Food was amazing! It was so many options my mind was blown. It’s all you can eat. And for extra $5 you can do the soup and add whatever you want. I’m thinking about making this a twice a month thing. For 2 people and 2 alcohol drinks the bill came up to $108. I think my sister was saying it’s about $30 a person. I tried some new stuff and some of my favorites.
